Use of нҗ№= н‘„ н‘„ 4ПҖнңҖ н‘ҹ or нҗ№= н‘ҳн‘„ н‘„ н‘ҹ нҗ№=11 N Example of calculation нҗ№= 79Г—1.6Г—10 вҲ’19 CГ—2Г—1.6Г—10 вҲ’19 C 4ПҖГ—8.85Г—10 вҲ’12 F m вҲ’1 Г— ( 5.68Г—10 вҲ’14 m ) =11.3 N (1) (1) Total for question 15 6 Questio n Number Answer Mark *16 This question assesses a studentвҖҷs ability to show a coherent and logically structured answer with linkages and fully-sustained reasoning. Marks are awarded for indicative content and for how the answer is structured and shows lines of reasoning. The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for indicative content. The following table shows how the marks should be awarded for structure and lines of reasoning. Indicative content IC1 There is a change in flux linkage (with the coil) Or the wires cut the magnetic field (lines) ignore magnet cuts lines no marks, but doesnвҖҷt prevent subsequent marks, e.g. for change in flux linkage. IC2 The greater the rate of change of flux linkage the larger the induced e.m.f. IC3 After the south pole reaches the coil the flux linkage (starts to) decrease Or When the south pole reaches the coil, by LenzвҖҷs law the polarity of the coil changes to continue to resist the motion of the magnet Or As the south pole reaches the coil the rate of change of flux linkage is zero (Not direction of field lines opposite)(reference to wires move along field lines for no change in flux linkage) IC4 As the south pole of the magnet passes through the coil the (induced) e.m.f. is negative IC5 The (downwards) speed of the magnet increases IC6 Emf is zero before magnet enters coil Or Emf is zero when midpoint of magnet in coil Or Maximum negative value is greater than maximum positive value Or Time for which emf is negative is greater than time for which emf is positive Or emf is zero when magnet totally leaves coil IC points IC mark Max linkage mark Max final mark 6 4 2 6 5 3 2 5 4 3 1 4 3 2 1 3 2 2 0 2 1 1 0 1 0 0 0 0 Number of marks awarded for structure of answer and sustained line of reasoning Answer shows a coherent and logical str

(By LenzвҖҷs law, if there were a complete circuit) the (direction of the) induced e.m.f. is such as to oppose the change that produces it (With a current) there would be a force to the right (opposing the motion) Or There would be a force in the direction opposite to the motion So e.m.f. is from P to Q (1) (1) (1) Total for question 17 6 Questio n Number Mark

Two corresponding pairs of values of V and t read from graph Use of н‘ү=н‘ү н‘’ вҲ’ н‘Ў н‘…нҗ¶ Or Use of ln V = ln V вҲ’ н‘Ў нҗ¶н‘… C= 497 (ОјF) (Range 463 ОјF to 520 ОјF) Comparison of calculated value to tolerance calculated using 10% and conclusion as to whether it is in tolerance Or use of difference between calculated and labelled value to calculate percentage difference and conclusion as to whether it is in tolerance OR Use of V = V /e (4.4 V) to find time constant (74 s) Or intercept with t axis using initial tangent to find time constant Use of time constant = RC C= 493 (ОјF) (Range 463 ОјF to 520 ОјF) Comparison of calculated value to tolerance calculated using 10% and conclusion as to whether it is in tolerance Or use of difference between calculated and labelled value to calculate percentage difference and conclusion as to whether it is in tolerance Example of calculation 4.1 V 12 V =e вҲ’ 80 s 150 Г— 10 О© Г— нҗ¶ нҗ¶=вҲ’ 80 s 150Г—10 О©Г—ln( 4.1 V 12 V ) =4.97Г—10 вҲ’4 F Largest C = 1.1 Г— 470 ОјF = 517 ОјF The capacitance is 497 ОјF which is less than the maximum value of 517 ОјF, so value is within tolerance (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1)

Use of н‘Ҡ= . н‘„ нҗ¶ Use of W = нҗ¶н‘ү Calculates ratio of energies stored and makes comparison to 1000 and suitable conclusion Or Applies factor of 1000 to one calculated energy and makes comparison to the other energy and suitable conclusion Example of calculation н‘Ҡ= . (56 C) 47 F =33.4 J W = Г—470 Г—10 вҲ’6 Г— ( 12 V ) =0.0338 J Ratio= 33.4 J 0.0338 J =987 Ratio of energies stored is 990 which is close to 1000, so claim is accurate (1) (1) (1) Total for question 18 7 Questio n Number Mark

Use of н‘қ=н‘ҡн‘Ј Use of trigonometrical function for x or y component of momentum for either stone Applies conservation of momentum in x direction or y direction v = 1.32 (m s вҲ’1 ) (3 sf reqd) if x components considered Or v = 1.33 (m s вҲ’1 ) (3 sf reqd) if y components considered Example of calculation н‘қ=19.1 kgГ—0.87 m s вҲ’1 =16.6 kg m s вҲ’1 н‘Ұ component for upper stone =16.6 kg m s вҲ’1 Г—sin50 o =12.7 kg m s вҲ’1 н‘Ұ component for lower stone=12.7 kg m s вҲ’1 = 19.1 kgГ— н‘Јsin30 o н‘Ј= 12.7 kg m s вҲ’1 0.5Г— 19.1 kg =1.33 m s вҲ’1 (1) (1) (1) (1)

Use of нҗё н‘ҳ = н‘ҡн‘Ј Or use of нҗё н‘ҳ = н‘қ 2н‘ҡ Correct calculation of one kinetic energy (e.c.f from (a)) Comparison and conclusion consistent with correctly calculated values of kinetic energy Example of calculation нҗё k = Г—19.1 kgГ—(1.7 m s вҲ’1 ) =27.6 J before нҗё k = Г—19.1 kgГ— ( 0.87 m s вҲ’1 ) + Г—19.1 kgГ— ( 1.33 m s вҲ’1 ) вҲҙнҗё н‘ҳ =7.2 J+16.9 J=24.1 J after Initial E k = 28 J so kinetic energy is not the same and collision is not elastic (1) (1) (1) Total for question 19 7 Questio n Number Mark

When the sphere touches the plate it is charged with the same polarity The force on the sphere due to the electric field is away from that plate so it moves towards the opposite plate Or the sphere is repelled from the plate with the charge of the same sign Or the sphere is attracted towards the plate with opposite charge When the sphere touches the charged plate opposite the first it becomes oppositely charged and is repelled from that charged plate (and so on) Or When the sphere touches the oppositely charged plate it becomes oppositely charged and is attracted to the first plate (and so on) (1) (1) (1)

(The bell connected to the lightning conductor becomes positively charged so) electrons are attracted to the right-hand side of the sphere The sphere is attracted to the positively charged bell [MP2 dependent on award of MP1] (1) (1) Total for question 20 10 Questio n Number Mark

There is a (resultant) force on the electrons in the vertical direction So the electrons are accelerated vertically But in the horizontal direction the electrons have a constant speed (1) (1) (1) Use of н‘Ҡ=н‘„н‘ү Use of нҗё K = н‘ҡн‘Ј н‘Ј=1.73Г—10 (m s вҲ’1 )(minimum 3 sf required) Example of calculation нҗё K =1.6Г—10 вҲ’19 CГ—850 V=1.36Г—10 вҲ’16 J н‘Ј= вҲҡ 2Г—1.6Г—10 вҲ’19 CГ—850 V 9.11Г—10 вҲ’31 kg =1.73Г—10 m s вҲ’1 (1) (1) (1) Use of н‘ =н‘ўн‘Ў Use of нҗ№=нҗён‘„ Use of нҗ№=н‘ҡн‘Һ Use of н‘ =н‘ўн‘Ў+ н‘Һн‘Ў н‘ =0.028 m (Allow ecf from (b)(i)) Example of calculation н‘Ў= 7.5 Г—10 вҲ’2 m 1.73Г—10 m s вҲ’1 =4.34Г—10 вҲ’9 s нҗ№=1.7Г—10 V m вҲ’1 Г—1.6Г—10 вҲ’19 C=2.72Г—10 вҲ’15 N н‘Һ= 2.72Г—10 вҲ’15 N 9.11Г—10 вҲ’31 kg =2.99Г—10 m s вҲ’2 н‘ = Г—2.99Г—10 m s вҲ’2 (4.34Г—10 вҲ’9 s) =0.028 m (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) ) Use of нҗ№=нҗөн‘„н‘Јsinнңғ with нҗ№= н‘ҡн‘Ј н‘ҹ to obtain н‘’ н‘ҡ = н‘Ј нҗөн‘ҹ Or Use of н‘қ=н‘ҡн‘Ј with н‘ҹ= н‘қ нҗөн‘„ to obtain н‘’ н‘ҡ = н‘Ј нҗөн‘ҹ н‘’ н‘ҡ =1.65Г—10 C kg вҲ’1 (ecf from (b)(i)) Substitutes standard values into н‘’ н‘ҡ Standard value of н‘’ н‘ҡ =1.76Г—10 C kg вҲ’1 calculated and comparison with experimental value and clear conclusion Example of calculation н‘’ н‘ҡ = 1.73Г—10 m s вҲ’1 3.0Г—10 вҲ’3 TГ—3.5Г—10 вҲ’2 m =1.65Г—10 C kg вҲ’1 н‘’ н‘ҡ = 1.6Г—10 вҲ’19 C 9.11Г—10 вҲ’31 kg =1.76Г—10 C kg вҲ’1 (1) (1) (1) (1) Total for question 22 15 Pearson Education Limited.
